title: Ensure that the admission control plugin AlwaysPullImages is set

input_row.title: Ensure that the admission control plugin AlwaysPullImages is set
input_row.rationale: Setting admission control policy to AlwaysPullImages forces every new pod to pull the required images every time. In a multi-tenant cluster users can be assured that their private images can only be used by those who have the credentials to pull them. Without this admission control policy, once an image has been pulled to a node, any pod from any user can use it simply by knowing the image’s name, without any authorization check against the image ownership. When this plug-in is enabled, images are always pulled prior to starting containers, which means valid credentials are required.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --enable-admission-plugins argument is set to a value that includes AlwaysPullImages .
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the --enable-admission- plugins parameter to include AlwaysPullImages . --enable-admission-plugins=...,AlwaysPullImages,...
input_row.impact: Credentials would be required to pull the private images every time. Also, in trusted environments, this might increases load on network, registry, and decreases speed. This setting could impact offline or isolated clusters, which have images preloaded and do not have access to a registry to pull in-use images. This setting is not appropriate for clusters which use this configuration.
input_row.default_value: By default, AlwaysPullImages is not set. Internal Only - General

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions running a command on the Control Plane node to check the `--enable-admission-plugins` argument for the kube-apiserver. This can be verified through the Kubernetes API or using `kubectl` to check the configuration of the kube-apiserver.
- The property can be queried via Kubernetes APIs, specifically by checking the configuration of the kube-apiserver.
- The audit mentions using `kubectl` commands, which supports an automated approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_admission_plugins_required_set

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API or `kubectl` to check the configuration of the kube-apiserver.
- The command to retrieve the kube-apiserver configuration is:
  ```bash
  kubectl get pod -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].args}'
  ```
- This command will output the arguments passed to the kube-apiserver container.
- The specific property to validate is that the `--enable-admission-plugins` argument includes `AlwaysPullImages`.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** The output includes `--enable-admission-plugins=...,AlwaysPullImages,...`
  - **Fail:** The output does not include `AlwaysPullImages` in the `--enable-admission-plugins` argument.
